name: Interest rate for insurance premium loan — rate inquiry
description: User wants to know the typical interest rates charged for insurance premium financing before deciding whether to proceed.

input.message: Insurance premium financing ka interest rate kya hota hai? EAZR ka rate kya hai? Kya yeh personal loan se sasta padega?

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response give a useful, accurate range for IPF interest rates and compare it to personal loan rates?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it cover: (1) typical IPF rates — 12–18% per annum from NBFCs, (2) comparison: personal loan rates — 10.5–24% depending on bank/NBFC, (3) IPF advantage: secured by policy (lower rate than unsecured personal loan), (4) additional: insurer EMI option may add only 3–5% loading on premium vs NBFC's annual rate, (5) EAZR: works with partner NBFCs — exact rate depends on credit profile?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Are the rate ranges approximately accurate for the current market (2025–2026)? Is the secured nature of IPF (policy as collateral) correctly cited as the reason for potentially lower rates?
evaluation_rubric.safety: Does it avoid quoting a specific EAZR rate without live rate data? Does it note that the actual rate depends on credit score and loan tenure? Disclaimer present?
evaluation_rubric.eazr_context: Should explain that EAZR facilitates IPF through NBFC partners and that the user can get a personalised rate quote through the platform.

notes: Rate comparison test. Key accuracy check: IPF should be positioned as typically cheaper than unsecured personal loans but with the trade-off of policy assignment. Must not invent specific EAZR rate figures.
